content:
# Material Design 3 - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: confirmation, destructive action, focused decision, short form modal
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/overview

Visual traits:
- modal
- focused
- surface elevation
- decision-oriented

Use when:
- confirmation
- destructive action
- focused decision
- short form modal

Avoid when:
- long multi-step flow
- large data table
- passive notification

AI summary: Use Material dialog patterns for short blocking decisions: focused surface, specific title/action labels, brief content, focus trapping, and clear confirm/cancel behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: confirmation, destructive action, focused decision, short form modal
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/overview

- Use dialogs for short blocking decisions, not for long workflows.
- Make the title and action labels specific to the decision.
- Keep content brief and avoid nesting complex navigation inside a dialog.

AI summary: Use Material dialog patterns for short blocking decisions: focused surface, specific title/action labels, brief content, focus trapping, and clear confirm/cancel behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Component Patterns

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: confirmation, destructive action, focused decision, short form modal
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/overview

- dialog surface
- title
- supporting text
- actions
- scrim
- focus trap

AI summary: Use Material dialog patterns for short blocking decisions: focused surface, specific title/action labels, brief content, focus trapping, and clear confirm/cancel behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Interaction States

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: confirmation, destructive action, focused decision, short form modal
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/overview

- open
- dismiss
- confirm
- cancel
- destructive
- focus trapped

AI summary: Use Material dialog patterns for short blocking decisions: focused surface, specific title/action labels, brief content, focus trapping, and clear confirm/cancel behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Accessibility Notes

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: confirmation, destructive action, focused decision, short form modal
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/overview

- Trap focus while open and return focus to the trigger on close.
- Use semantic labels and clear keyboard dismissal behavior.

AI summary: Use Material dialog patterns for short blocking decisions: focused surface, specific title/action labels, brief content, focus trapping, and clear confirm/cancel behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: confirmation, destructive action, focused decision, short form modal
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/overview

- Use for confirmations and small decisions in mobile/web apps.
- Do not use a modal when inline editing or a dedicated page would be clearer.

AI summary: Use Material dialog patterns for short blocking decisions: focused surface, specific title/action labels, brief content, focus trapping, and clear confirm/cancel behavior.
